subject: traffic
post: love how my commute turns into a full meditation session because the car in front hasn't moved in ten minutes. truly peak urban life experience.

subject: C/o Kancharapalem
post: Finally watched C/o Kancharapalem and honestly, it’s just pure heart. No big stars, no forced drama, just people living their lives in a way that feels so real. Wish more mainstream movies took notes on how to write simple, honest characters.

subject: online grocery delivery
post: nothing humbles you faster than realizing you ordered five packets of snacks but forgot to buy actual food for the week.

subject: Thithi (Kannada film)
post: still thinking about Thithi. it’s rare to find a movie that’s so funny and cynical at the same time without trying too hard. the way the entire village acts like it’s a documentary is just wild.

subject: family group chats
post: the 4 AM 'good morning' flower picture in the family group chat is a bold choice for someone who hasn't spoken to me since Diwali.

subject: Aavesham movie
post: Finally watched Aavesham and honestly Fahadh Faasil just carries the whole thing on his back. That shift in character midway through caught me so off guard, absolute madness.

subject: AI coding tools debugging
post: spent an hour fighting with an AI suggestion that looked perfect but had the most subtle logic error imaginable. sometimes it saves me half a day, other times it just gaslights me into thinking my own code is broken. ugh.

subject: procrastination
post: tell myself i’ll start this task in exactly ten minutes, then look up and realize two hours of random wikipedia rabbit holes have vanished. at this point i’m just gaslighting my own productivity.

subject: daytime mood shift
post: the 4pm crash is hitting different today, bas chai mil jaye toh thoda insaan jaisa feel karu

subject: AR Rahman and ARR songs
post: nothing hits quite like late 90s AR Rahman at 2 AM. just put on a random playlist and ended up listening to the Roja and Dil Se albums on loop. sach mein, the production on these tracks is still lightyears ahead of anything coming out today.

subject: gym motivation
post: my gym motivation usually lasts exactly until the moment i see a cloud outside and decide that walking to the car is too much of a workout for one day.

subject: sleep schedule struggle
post: why is my brain suddenly so active at 2am when i have to be up in four hours. just shut up and let me sleep yaar

subject: autocorrect
post: my phone really thinks i am constantly trying to text people about 'ducking' things. trust me, i have much more creative ways to be frustrated in real life.

subject: The Family Man series
post: Started rewatching The Family Man and somehow it feels even better the second time around. Manoj Bajpayee is just top tier, nobody plays that 'stressed middle-class guy' role quite like him. Srikant Tiwari is a whole mood.

subject: small talk
post: it’s always the same three questions about my life and then five minutes of silence while we both wait for the other person to be the first to leave. pure cinema.

subject: weather mood
post: it finally rained and the whole vibe of the room just changed. honestly might just sit here and do nothing for an hour instead of trying to be productive.

subject: Maheshinte Prathikaaram
post: Maheshinte Prathikaaram is basically the best therapy movie ever. That scene where he just decides to not wear chappals until he takes revenge is peak cinema, man. It’s so grounded, you just want to sit in that Idukki vibe forever.

subject: sleep
post: my brain at 2 AM really decided it’s the perfect time to audit every embarrassing thing i’ve said since 2012. so helpful, truly.

subject: grocery store decision fatigue
post: stood in the supermarket for ten minutes straight just staring at the different brands of curd because i couldn't remember which one my mom told me to buy. itna confusion bas dahi lene mein, kya hi kar raha hu main life mein

subject: Kishore Kumar songs
post: Was having such a mid Monday until I put on some Kishore Kumar classics. There's something about his voice that just makes everything feel a little less chaotic. Perfect way to deal with the office headache.

subject: Hey Ram
post: arre, rewatched Hey Ram today. Kamal Haasan’s vision is actually insane, feel like people talk about the technical side so much they miss how gut-wrenching the personal story is. that ending just stays with you for days.

subject: small act of kindness
post: Saw an uncle help a street dog cross the road in this crazy traffic today and honestly it made my entire day better. Little things like that are the only reason to stay sane.

subject: Anirudh Ravichander music
post: that beat drop in the Leo soundtrack always gets me hyped even if it’s just me sitting at my desk. Anirudh really doesn't know how to make a boring track, the energy is just next level.

subject: random childhood nostalgia
post: found an old keychain in my bag today and it hit me how fast time is moving. feels like i was in school just yesterday and now here i am dealing with taxes and bills. itna jaldi sab kuch badal gaya.

subject: Kumbalangi Nights
post: that one scene in Kumbalangi Nights where they’re just sitting on the roof watching the lights across the water—it’s been years and I still think about it whenever I need to just zone out. Malayalam cinema really knows how to make you feel like you're actually there.

subject: Mathu Vadalara
post: Mathu Vadalara is easily one of the most fun thrillers I’ve seen in a while. The whole plot happening in one night with those guys just stuck in a house is hilarious and stressful at the same time. Really hope more people experiment with scripts like this.

subject: food delivery
post: the delivery app saying 'your order is being prepared' for 40 minutes is just a fancy way of saying my dinner is currently a concept, not a reality.

subject: evening traffic exhaustion
post: stuck in this gachibowli traffic for an hour and i’m just staring at the tail lights at this point. sach mein, bas ghar pahunch ke bed pe girna hai aur kuch nahi chahiye.

subject: Arijit Singh and indie music
post: that feeling when you switch from a heavy playlist to an Arijit acoustic track and suddenly everything slows down. sometimes you just need that one song to clear your head after a long day in this humidity.

subject: meetings
post: the best part of a 60-minute meeting that could have been a three-line email is realizing you could have finished your entire week's to-do list while listening to people say 'let's circle back' for the tenth time.

subject: Iyobinte Pusthakam
post: Iyobinte Pusthakam is something else man, the cinematography alone makes you feel like you're standing in those misty Munnar hills. Fahaad and Lal are just monsters in this. Such a dark, beautiful period drama that doesn't get enough love outside Kerala.

subject: Panchayat series
post: Man, watching Panchayat feels like such a reset. Phulera is the kind of calm I didn’t know I needed right now. Sach mein, the simplicity of the writing just hits different compared to all the high-stakes thrillers I’ve been binging lately.
